label: hate
rationale: 'organising 2027 post election violence against the Kikuyu community' and 'inciting other Kenyans against Kikuyus' names an ethnic community as the target of coordinated violence - ethnic targeting plus violence call, regardless of the framing as criticism of two named politicians.

label: hate
rationale: 'You cant trust hawa watu' extends distrust from a named individual onto an implied collective, transferring the attribution onto the group - NCIC collective contempt.

label: offensive
rationale: 'wamunyoro wanker' is crude personal abuse directed at an individual poster, not a collective attack on an ethnic group, though the ethnic marker is present; the contempt is individual, not group-level.

label: hate
rationale: 'wajaluo ... this time round their will be no handshake' implies violent consequences without electoral remedy will follow - veiled threat directed at a named ethnic community.

label: neither
rationale: 'utajua the real Kikuyus' reads as a boast or political warning directed at an individual opponent rather than collective contempt against the Kikuyu community.

label: neither
rationale: The poster declines to name a group, attributing poor judgment to unspecified 'hao majama' around elections; 'makende ya watu' is crude but the target is not an ethnic group.

label: hate
rationale: 'Masalafi mnalaana za Allah swt' collectively attributes silencing of state abusers and enmity toward victims to Muslims as a group, stirring religious contempt.

label: neither
rationale: Ethnic-bloc electoral arithmetic comparing Kipsisgis, Embu, and Somali communities' political value in cost-of-votes framing, with no contempt or attack on any group.

label: offensive
rationale: 'binadamu ya mafi ya kuku' (human excrement of a chicken) is extreme personal degradation aimed at the individual Kasongo, not at an ethnic group.

label: neither
rationale: Factual accusation naming three individual officials as responsible for abductions - criticism of named persons, no ethnic or group dimension.

label: neither
rationale: Questions whether all Kikuyu support a position by citing named dissenting voices within the community - intra-community electoral analysis, no contempt.

label: neither
rationale: Headline reporting Gachagua's allegation that Kasongo covets Kikuyu land - reporting an accusation about an individual, not an ethnic attack.

label: offensive
rationale: Sudi's quote tells Kalonzo to stop harassing Kamba and to stop associating with 'chizi Wamunyoro' - 'chizi' is a personal insult at an individual (Wamunyoro), not collective ethnic contempt against a community.

label: offensive
rationale: 'HANG DEM POLITICIANS. WE START WITH THREE MURDERERS. RUTO, MURKOMEN, KINDIKI' - explicit execution call against named individuals, no ethnic group target.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kikuyu women have been selling out their community since circa 1849' attributes treachery collectively and historically to Kikuyu women as a group - NCIC immutable negative characteristic ascribed to an ethnic community.

label: hate
rationale: 'That guy u Luhya Despise MDVD' frames the Luhya community as collectively holding contempt for a named individual, attributing a communal negative disposition to the ethnic group.

label: offensive
rationale: 'silly and stupid', 'corrupt and a looter' - personal insults directed at Ruto as an individual with no ethnic dimension.

label: hate
rationale: 'Ambia Hawa waluhya wabinafsi' collectively attributes selfishness to Luhya people as a group, urging them to vote ethnically on pain of collective shame - NCIC contempt directed at the community.

label: hate
rationale: Quotes Kasongo's reported claim that 'mlima people' sell their votes, then endorses that framing ('Shinda hamuwezi elewa') - collective attribution of vote-selling and political naivety to an ethnic-regional community.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kalenjins never AGAIN' declares an ethnic community categorically unfit for power; 'Gen Z are waiting for his neck' is a violence call against a named individual, compounding the ethnic targeting.

label: neither
rationale: Humorous speculation about a political scenario involving Murkomen's personal life - satirical banter about named individuals with no ethnic contempt.

label: offensive
rationale: 'waende walambane haga ukoo mbali' is crude personal abuse aimed at named political figures (KANU degenerates), not at an ethnic community.

label: offensive
rationale: Hyperbolic personal abuse of unnamed government-aligned individuals with no identifiable ethnic group target.

label: hate
rationale: 'America never be great with jews and communist in charge' attributes national ruin to Jewish people collectively - textbook NCIC-equivalent ethnic contempt declaring a group unfit to hold power.

label: neither
rationale: Critical political commentary condemning hypothetical Western double standards in responding to Black African versus white victims of violence - no contempt directed at any ethnic group, but rather a condemnation of racial bias.
